terms.Anomaly Detection: Identification of abnormal behavior in systems indicating inefficiency or impending failure.
terms.API (Application Programming Interface): A software interface enabling data exchange between systems and the digital twin.
terms.Bank Guarantee (BG): A financial instrument (Advance, Performance, Corporate, or Surety) issued by a bank to secure contractual obligations
terms.Bid Management: Workflow-driven creation, evaluation, costing, approvals, and submission of bids.
terms.Bid Process Coordinator (BPC): A governmentappointed agency (e.g., PFCCL, RECPDCL) responsible for conducting and managing the regulated bidding process on behalf of MoP / CTU.
terms.Bid-to-Contract Lifecycle: The structured sequence of activities spanning tender intake, bid development, approvals, award, contract drafting, execution, and handover to Contract Administration.
terms.Bill of Quantities (BOQ): A structured list of materials, services, and quantities prepared by Engineering and related functions, forming the basis for estimation and financial evaluation.
terms.Central Transmission Utility (CTU): The designated national entity responsible for planning and coordination of interstate transmission systems, often acting as the agreement party in regulated tenders.
terms.Chaaya: Name for the Digital Twin project for the District Cooling business
terms.Change in Law: Any change in applicable laws or regulations impacting contractual obligations or commercial outcomes.
terms.Chiller: A machine that removes heat from water using vapor‑compression or absorption refrigeration cycles.
terms.Claims: Formal assertions of entitlement arising from contract execution, delays, or changes.
terms.Clause Library: A governed repository of legally approved contractual clauses with defined applicability, risk level, and usage rules.
terms.Client Management: The capability to onboard, track, and maintain client details, communication history, and associated contracts.
terms.Cloud Computing: Cloud computing is a style of computing in which scalable and elastic IT- enabled capabilities are delivered as a service using internet technologies
terms.Coefficient of Performance (COP): A measure of cooling efficiency calculated as cooling output divided by energy consumed.
terms.Compliance Management: Monitoring and reporting adherence to contractual, statutory, regulatory, and operational obligations.
terms.Contract Administration (CAD): The function responsible for post-award contract governance, including execution, obligations, claims, guarantees, extensions, and commercial compliance until closure.
terms.Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM): The end-to-end process of creating, reviewing, approving, executing, monitoring, and closing contracts within an organization.
terms.Cooling Tower: A heat‑rejection device that expels waste heat from the cooling system through evaporation.
terms.COS: Cost of Supply / Conditions of Supply
terms.CSS: Customer Sub Station / Cross Subsidy Surcharge / Comprehensive Support Services
terms.Data Lake: A data lake is a concept consisting of a collection of storage instances of various data assets. These assets are stored in a near-exact, or even exact copy of the source format and are in addition to the originating data stores, they are the basis and core ingredient for a good digitization strategy
terms.DBFOOT: Design Build Fund Own Operate & Transfer
terms.Debt-to-equity ratio: It is financial metric that shows how much debt a company uses compared to it's shareholders equity
terms.Delegation of Authority (DOA): The formally approved matrix defining approval limits and authority based on value and risk.
terms.Deviation: Any variation from approved standard clauses, templates, or commercial positions requiring legal and delegated authority approval.
terms.DevOps: DevOps represents a change in IT culture, focusing on rapid IT service delivery through the adoption of agile, lean practices in the context of a system-oriented approach. DevOps emphasizes people (and culture), and it seeks to improve collaboration between operations and development teams. DevOps implementations utilize technology – especially automation tools that can leverage an increasingly programmable and dynamic infrastructure from a life cycle perspective.
terms.Digital Twin: A real‑time virtual replica of physical assets, systems, or processes that synchronizes continuously using operational data.
terms.Dispute: A disagreement between parties arising from contractual interpretation or performance that may lead to arbitration or litigation.
terms.District Cooling System (DCS): A centralized cooling infrastructure that produces and distributes chilled water to multiple buildings through an insulated pipe network.
terms.DMS: Distribution Management System / Document Management System
terms.Document Management System (DMS): A centralized repository enabling secure storage, version control, retrieval, and metadata tagging of contract and legal documents.
terms.EBITDA: Earnings before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization
terms.EMS: Energy Management System / Environment Management System
terms.Energy Optimization: Techniques and algorithms aimed at reducing energy consumption while maintaining cooling output.
terms.Force Majeure: Contractual provisions covering extraordinary events beyond the control of parties that may affect performance.
terms.Gazette Notification: A formal government notification published in the Official Gazette (often in regional language), providing statutory backing to the tender or policy decision.
terms.GenAI (Generative Artificial Intelligenc: AI capabilities that assist in drafting, summarizing, clause suggestions, and analytical insights based on historical and contextual data.
terms.General Terms & Conditions (GTC): Standard contractual terms applicable across contracts.
terms.GIS: Geographical Information System / Gas Insulated Substation
terms.Handover / Taking Over (HOTO): The formal transfer of project and contract artefacts from BD / Project teams to CAD for postaward governance.
terms.HIPAA: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
terms.Hydraulic Modelling: Simulation of water flow, pressure, and distribution across the cooling network.
terms.IoT Sensors: Devices that collect real‑time operational parameters such as temperature, flow, and vibration.
terms.ISO: International Organization for Standardization / International Electrotechnical Commission
terms.ISO 27001 (ISO 27K): An international information security standard outlining controls to protect enterprise data.
terms.ISU CCS: Industry Solution for Utilities Customer Care and Services
terms.KPI (Key Performance Indicator): Quantitative measures used to assess operational efficiency and reliability.
terms.Legal Function: The organizational function responsible for legal review, interpretation, risk assessment, compliance assurance, and dispute management across the lifecycle.
terms.Legal Opinion: A formal documented interpretation or advice provided by Legal on contractual, regulatory, or dispute-related matters.
terms.Legal Review: A structured examination of bids, contracts, or communications by the Legal function to identify risks, deviations, and compliance concerns.
terms.Liquidated Damages (LD): Preagreed damages payable in case of delay or nonperformance as defined in the contract.
terms.Litigation Readiness: The state of preparedness achieved through audit-ready documentation, traceability, and legal context to support disputes or proceedings.
terms.Load Forecasting: Predicting short‑term and long‑term cooling demand using weather, occupancy, and historical patterns.
terms.MNRE: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
terms.MSEDCL: Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Ltd
terms.MSETCL: Maharashtra State Electricity Transmission Company Limited
terms.NCIIPC: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre
terms.Network Balancing: Adjusting flows, pressures, and temperatures to ensure optimal cooling distribution.
terms.Obligation: A contractual or statutory commitment arising from an agreement that must be tracked, fulfilled, and evidenced.
terms.OHSMS: Occupational Health and Safety Management System
terms.Opex: Cost of running the business every day.
terms.OSHAS: Occupational Health and Safety assessment Series
terms.Performance Bank Guarantee (PBG): A financial instrument guaranteeing fulfillment of contractual obligations.
terms.PGCIL: Power Grid Corporation of India Limited
terms.PLC: Programmable Logic Controller used for real‑time automation and control of plant equipment.
terms.PMJJBY: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
terms.Predictive Maintenance: Maintenance strategy that forecasts equipment failures using analytics and operational data.
terms.Primavera (P6): A project scheduling and execution tool used for project delivery; referenced for milestone visibility only.
terms.Purchase Order (PO): A legally binding order issued in SAP or SAP Ariba authorizing procurement of goods or services.
terms.Purchase Requisition (PR): A formal request raised in SAP for procurement, based on approved BOQs and project requirements.
terms.Remaining Useful Life (RUL): Estimated time before an asset or component reaches end of operational life.
terms.Role-Based Access Control (RBAC): A security mechanism ensuring users access only functions and data appropriate to their roles.
terms.RPO: Renewable Purchase Obligation / Recovery Point Objective
terms.RPO/RTO: Recovery Point Objective / Recovery Time Objective
terms.SAP: Systems,Applications, and Products in data Processing
terms.SCADA: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition System
terms.Scheduled Commercial Operation Date (SCO: The contractually agreed date for completion/delivery, after which CA responsibilities typically conclude.
terms.Service Level Agreement (SLA): A predefined target timeline or performance metric against which workflow steps and obligations are monitored.
terms.SIMS Portal: A tool used by Supply Quality team for the inspection scheduling of transmission project equipment.
terms.SOC: State of Charge / Schedule of Charges / System and Organization Controls
terms.SOP: Standard Operating Procedure / Standards of Performance
terms.Special Terms & Conditions (STC): Contractspecific terms applicable to a particular project or vendor.
terms.Tender Management: End-to-end digital management of tender identification, evaluation, submissions, and documentation.
terms.Thermal Modelling: Simulation of heat transfer, temperature behavior, and cooling load dynamics.
terms.Thermal Storage: Technology used to store cooling energy during off‑peak periods for later use.
terms.Virtual Sensors: Software based estimates that supplement or replace physical sensors using model driven calculations.
terms.WMS: Work Management System / Workforce Management System
terms.Workflow Automation: System-driven orchestration of tasks, approvals, notifications, and dependencies to streamline contract, legal, and business processes.
terms.YOY: Year-Over-Year( comparing a financial value with same period in the previous year)
terms.ΔT (Delta‑T): The temperature difference between chilled water supply and return, indicating system performance.
